div.p: Malcolm king of Scots to his Justices, Sheriffs, Provosts, Officials, and all other worthy men of the whole of his land, Greeting: I command and firmly enjoin that in whosesoever land or power the abbot of Holyrood is able to find neyfs of his own land or his own fugitives, he should rightly have them. And no one may withhold any of them from him or his servant, on pain of my full forfeiture. As witnesses, Arnold bishop of St Andrews, John abbot of Kelso, Geoffrey abbot of Dunfermline. At Maidens’ Castle.
